title: Historical Case: 2017 Equifax Breach
content:
LESSONS FROM EQUIFAX: Struts CVE-2017-5638
в•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђв•ђ

INCIDENT OVERVIEW:
March-July 2017: Attackers exploited unpatched
Apache Struts vulnerability (CVE-2017-5638) to
compromise Equifax systems.

IMPACT SCALE:
вЂў 147 million consumers affected
вЂў Social Security numbers exposed
вЂў Birth dates, addresses compromised
вЂў Credit card data for 209,000 stolen
вЂў Settlement: $700 million

ATTACK TIMELINE:
March 7: Apache releases Struts patch
March 10: Equifax vulnerability notice
May 13: Initial breach (undetected)
July 29: Breach discovered (76 days late)
Sept 7: Public disclosure

ROOT CAUSES:
вќЊ Patch not applied for 2+ months
вќЊ Vulnerability scanner missed the issue
вќЊ SSL certificate expired (monitoring gap)
вќЊ Segmentation failures
вќЊ Inadequate logging

KEY TAKEAWAYS:
вњ“ Patch critical CVEs within 48 hours
вњ“ Verify scanner coverage quarterly
вњ“ Monitor certificate expiry
вњ“ Implement defense in depth
вњ“ Enable comprehensive audit logging
